rules.name: Part B Community Need Evidence
rules.description: Funding requests of type new-operational-funding, expanded-services, or capital-maintenance (Part B) require the communityNeedEvidence field to be populated before the request can be submitted.
rules.message: Community need evidence is required for Part B funding requests

rules.name: Submission Window
rules.description: A funding request may only transition to submitted status during the configured submission window for its fiscal year. The window open and close dates are stored in SystemConfiguration.

rules.name: Organization Eligibility
rules.description: An organization must have been established for at least one year (current year minus yearEstablished >= 1) before it may submit a funding request.
rules.message: Organization must be established for at least 1 year before submitting a funding request

rules.name: Legal Representative Required
rules.description: Every organization must have a contact with contactRole = 'legal_representative' before a funding request can be submitted.
rules.condition: Organization must have at least one contact where contactRole = legal_representative
rules.message: A legal representative contact is required for the organization

rules.name: Document Validation
rules.description: Supporting documents must pass file type validation (permitted MIME types), maximum file size limits, and virus scanning before they become available for download. Documents with virusScanStatus = 'infected' are quarantined and cannot be downloaded.

rules.name: Funding Request State Machine
rules.description: The funding request lifecycle follows a strict state machine. Transitions are role-gated and condition-dependent. Only forward transitions are allowed (no skipping stages). Withdrawal is permitted from any non-terminal state.

conditions: BR-001: Part B evidence present if applicable
Request has been assigned to an analyst
To evaluated: assessment must be finalized with composite score
Applicant has responded to the information request
Manager has reviewed the finalized assessment
BR-011: Decision sequence respected (preliminary before executive before final)
BR-010: Approved amount does not exceed requested amount

rules.name: Role-Based Access Control
rules.description: All API operations enforce role-based access. Citizens (applicant role) may only access their own organization's data. Staff roles are hierarchical: analysts can assess, managers can decide (preliminary), leadership and executive-office can decide (final). System-admin manages users and roles.

rules.name: Funded Beds for Emergency Housing Programs
rules.description: Programs with programType = 'emergency-housing' must specify a fundedBeds value greater than zero.
rules.message: Funded beds count is required for emergency housing programs

rules.name: Approved Amount Ceiling
rules.description: The approvedAmount on a DecisionRecord must not exceed the requestedFundingAmount on the associated FundingRequest.

rules.name: Decision Sequence
rules.description: Decision types must follow a strict sequence: preliminary, then executive_review, then final. An executive_review decision requires a prior preliminary decision. A final decision requires a prior executive_review decision.

rules.name: Filter Notes Required
rules.description: When a filter analysis response answer is 'no' or 'more-info', the analyst must provide explanatory notes.
rules.message: Notes are required when answer is 'no' or 'more-info'

rules.name: Composite Score Formula
rules.description: The assessment composite score is a weighted average of three dimension scores: community need (40%), organization capacity (30%), and service gap (30%). The result is rounded to two decimal places.

rules.name: Privacy Suppression
rules.description: LGA indicator score values for communities with population below 40 are replaced with a proxy value of 4.5 to prevent re-identification of individuals in small communities.

rules.name: Zero Substitution
rules.description: When an LGA indicator raw value is null (missing data), the score value is substituted with zero during RegionData ingestion.

rules.name: Immutable Audit Trail
rules.description: Once an audit entry is created, it cannot be modified or deleted. No UPDATE or DELETE operations are permitted on AuditEntry records.

rules.name: Seven-Year Retention
rules.description: All audit records must be retained for a minimum of seven years from the action timestamp, in compliance with government records management policy.
